title: Error::Wh has no human-readable name lookup for WH_ERROR_* codes
description: Error::Wh { code: i32 } formats as 'wolfHSM error -2104'. Users filing issues will copy the raw integer; maintainers must grep wolfhsm/wh_error.h to translate -2104 to WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND. All other named error conditions (AlreadyRegistered, BadArgs, DataLost, InvalidSignature) have English descriptions. The WH_ERROR_NOTFOUND value is already special-cased as a private constant in nvm.rs but is not exposed to users. Every new user who hits a server-side error and files an issue will open with 'I got error -XXXX; what does that mean?' — a class of issues that is completely avoidable.
